segments.text: What I am saying is that as long as an heir is underage, he is no different from a slave, although he owns the whole estate.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: The heir is subject to guardians and trustees until the time set by his father.
So also, when we were underage, we were in slavery under the elemental spiritual forces of the world.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But when the set time had fully come, God sent his Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
to redeem those under the law, that we might receive adoption to sonship.
Because you are his sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts,
Abba, Father
segments.speaker: The Spirit

segments.text: So you are no longer a slave, but God's child; and since you are his child, God has made you also an heir.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Formerly, when you did not know God, you were slaves to those who by nature are not gods.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: But now that you know God—or rather are known by God—how is it that you are turning back to those weak and miserable forces? Do you wish to be enslaved by them all over again?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: You are observing special days and months and seasons and years!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I fear for you, that somehow I have wasted my efforts on you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I plead with you, brothers and sisters, become like me, for I became like you. You did me no wrong.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: As you know, it was because of an illness that I first preached the gospel to you,
and even though my illness was a trial to you, you did not treat me with contempt or scorn. Instead, you welcomed me as if I were an angel of God, as if I were Christ Jesus himself.
Where, then, is your blessing of me now? I can testify that, if you could have done so, you would have torn out your eyes and given them to me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Have I now become your enemy by telling you the truth?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Those people are zealous to win you over, but for no good. What they want is to alienate you from us, so that you may have zeal for them.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: It is fine to be zealous, provided the purpose is good, and to be so always, not just when I am with you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: My dear children, for whom I am again in the pains of childbirth until Christ is formed in you,
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: how I wish I could be with you now and change my tone, because I am perplexed about you!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Tell me, you who want to be under the law, are you not aware of what the law says?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman and the other by the free woman.
His son by the slave woman was born according to the flesh, but his son by the free woman was born as the result of a divine promise.
These things are being taken figuratively: The women represent two covenants. One covenant is from Mount Sinai and bears children who are to be slaves: This is Hagar.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Now Hagar stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present city of Jerusalem, because she is in slavery with her children.
But the Jerusalem that is above is free, and she is our mother.
Be glad, barren woman, you who never bore a child; shout for joy and cry aloud, you who were never in labor; because more are the children of the desolate woman than of her who has a husband.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now you, brothers and sisters, like Isaac, are children of promise.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: At that time the son born according to the flesh persecuted the son born by the power of the Spirit. It is the same now.
Get rid of the slave woman and her son, for the slave woman's son will never share in the inheritance with the free woman's son.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore, brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman, but of the free woman.
segments.speaker: Paul
